section.title: Grades
section.p: General PCC Grading Guidelines are found at .

paragraphs.title: WeBWorK
paragraphs.p: Most of your homework for this class is submitted online through the free program . For details, see . Collectively, all web assignments are worth 20 of your final grade.

Unless a exercise explicitly asks you to use technology (a graphing app, or a CAS like SageMath) the expectation is that you do all of the exercise by hand. Don't deny yourself the chance to practice these exercises by handon exams you will have to complete the exercises by hand too.

paragraphs.title: Written Assignments
paragraphs.p: Exercises from the textbook (and perhaps occasionally from somewhere else) will be assigned to you to write up formally. The priority with these is to practice and develop your writing and explanation skills. Collectively, these exercises are worth 20. See for the specifics.
You may turn in written assignments in person, or upload your work to D2L.

If your score on an exercise from a written assignment is less than 3/4 and you are unhappy with it, you may redo that exercise from scratch and turn it in again, up to two weeks following the original due date. (By from scratch, I meant that you may not take your graded first attempt and scratch out things, pencil in more things, etc. Make a clean new attempt.)

paragraphs.title: Late Homework
paragraphs.p: assignments are due whenever says they are due. If you need an extension, you can ask me about that and it's usually no problem.
Written assignments have due dates to help you pace yourself and not become overwhelmed. If you need to turn in a written assignment late, that is OK as long as it is less than two weeks late.

If you find that you are routinely late with assignments, it is a sign that you may be at risk for not passing the course. And I would recommend looking into your weekly schedule to find a different routine.

paragraphs.title: Exams
paragraphs.p: Please refer to the for exam dates, and for details about what is covered on each exam. Each of the four exams is worth 15 of your grade.
